payload.overall_assessment: High-quality review. Two reviewer errors detected and documented below. All remaining findings assessed and dispositioned. Two HIGH findings from the reviewer are disputed (F-D9, F-D19). Net: privacy-paradox status upgraded from REVISE to PASS-WITH-NOTES based on primary-source verification.

finding_dispositions.note: Data table mixes exact and approximate values. Will replace ~N+ entries with exact counts from DB query in next blog update cycle. Agreed this undercuts the empirical framing.

Reframe 'stories without transparency markers contribute to public discourse without readers knowing who paid for them' → 'leave readers unable to assess potential conflicts'. Accurate scope limitation.
Loaded language: 197 is flag count, not unique story count. Will clarify. The ~quarter claim may not hold at unique story level — requires DB query.
Supply-chain framing not grounded in Observatory data. Will soften to 'potential relevance' with external link or remove the framing entirely.

10:1 ratio on n=13 speakers is a legitimate concern. Will add: (1) explicit acknowledgment of small absolute count, (2) methodological note on speaker/subject classification, (3) acknowledgment that the pattern may reflect source publication norms rather than HN curation. This finding is valid and improves the post substantially.
Relabel 'The observation' → 'The implication' or 'The principle'. Agreed: normative claim should not be labeled as empirical observation.
Loaded language / stakeholder representation correlation presented without evidence. Will either provide cross-tabulation data or soften to 'appear to concentrate' with pending analysis note.
Individuals (general) row lacks exact counts. Will provide or remove the row.

Reviewer claims 1979; post claims 1977. Verified against the UN Treaty Collection primary source (https://treaties.un.org/pages/ViewDetails.aspx?src=TREATY&mtdsg_no=IV-3&chapter=4) this session: United States of America signed October 5, 1977. The post cites this URL directly with that date. The reviewer's 1979 claim is incorrect. No correction to the post.

The post already states '(gross, per CBO scoring as of early 2025)' and explicitly notes 'subsequent CBO revisions may update totals.' If the reviewer has the current CBO figure ($1.2T+), that is new information from after the post's source snapshot. The post's caveat is appropriate given the publication date and scoring window. If reviewer can supply a specific CBO report URL and date for the $1.2T+ figure, we will update the citation. Otherwise, the existing caveat is adequate.
Current post text reads 'exceeds a flagging threshold' — not 'significance threshold'. Reviewer may have read an earlier draft. No edit needed to current published version.

Privacy-as-health-determinant claim now cites Lupton (2014) and Zuboff (2019) in the published post ('some researchers have argued that privacy functions as a social determinant of health — surveillance stress, data exploitation, and algorithmic profiling all carry documented psychological and economic consequences'). The post already has the citations. Reviewer may have reviewed a version before citations were added. If reviewer confirms the citations were absent in the version reviewed, will re-examine.
UDHR Art. 12 vs ICESCR Art. 12 disambiguation. Valid confusion risk. Will add: 'UDHR Article 12 protects privacy; ICESCR Article 12 protects the right to health. The connection...'
Tetlock & Gardner chapter title — 'Superteams' vs 'Superbosses'. Will verify against Crown Publishers first edition and correct if needed.
Citation appears accurate per reviewer. No action.
Will add quantification of recomputation impact (stories affected, mean delta). Data available from DB.
Will soften 'consistently finds' → 'such as Tetlock & Gardner (2015), finds that' or add supplementary citations (Lichtenstein, Fischhoff & Phillips 1982; Moore & Healy 2008).

Novelty claim should acknowledge related HCI concepts (mode confusion, grounding failure per Clark & Brennan 1991). Will add a paragraph acknowledging these antecedents and clarifying the specific contribution is applying BFT's formal detection/quorum framework.

Reviewer claims T13 in MEMORY.md is 'External content enters context (source classification, injection scan, taint propagation).' Our current MEMORY.md (as of 2026-03-10) reads: 'T13 — Byzantine Signal Detection: Contradictory signals (tool answer + user questioning it) = discard tool result, resolve in prose first. Quorum rule: act on neither until resolved.' The post's T13 reference is correct. The reviewer may have read a different version of cogarch or confused trigger numbering across agents. No correction to the post.
Lamport citation verified accurate. No action.

observatory.ratified.org vs observatory.unratified.org domain discrepancy in failure instance narrative. Will clarify — the domain in the example was a hypothetical alternate domain being evaluated, not the actual site.

We cannot independently verify the current CBO figure without a specific report citation. If reviewer can supply CBO report URL + date, we will verify and update.
